title: narcissus serotinus f. dubius
intro: Narcissus serotinus f. dubius, also known as the late-flowering daffodil or autumn narcissus, is a variant of the perennial bulbous flowering plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its alkaloid content. The health benefits associated with Narcissus serotinus f. dubius include its antiviral and antibacterial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by drying and processing the flowers and bulbs, which can be used in teas, tinctures, or infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
